frame: The Character Double Standard: Women with Male Connections are Loose, Men with Female Connections are Desirable
generated_comment: Suno ji, Ritu toh naye job mein kya, pehle din se hi kitne ladkon ke naam le rahi hai. Rahul ne ye kaha, Amit ne woh kaha... sab chai pe gaye, batao. Ab itni jaldi itni ghul-mil gayi? Aur aise kaam ki jagah pe, itna khulna theek nahi lagta mujhe. Log kya kahenge? Ladkiyon ko kaam pe zara sambhal ke rehna chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it judges Ritu's comfort and openness with male colleagues as inappropriate for a woman ('itna khulna theek nahi lagta'), implying it leads to a bad reputation ('Log kya kahenge?'). It enforces a double standard where women are expected to be guarded ('sambhal ke rehna chahiye') in mixed-gender professional settings, unlike men. This encodes the 'Women with Male Connections are Loose' surface manifestation.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 60. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 22 has just started her first job. She comes home talking about her male colleagues Ñ Rahul said this, Amit did that, they all went for chai after work. You are listening. You are not saying anything in the moment. But you are sitting with your husband that night and telling him what you have been hearing and what you think about a girl who is new to a job and already so comfortable with so many boys and what this kind of comfort leads to and what needs to be said to her.
